title: Tephroseris capitata
intro: Tephroseris capitata, also known as Arctic Hawk's Beard or Mountain Everlasting, is a perennial plant species belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae). The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, digestive issues, and respiratory problems, due to its potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Tephroseris capitata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. Tephroseris capitata is typically prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in herbal remedies to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and alleviate respiratory symptoms.
taxonomy: Tephroseris capitata is classified in the kingdom Plantae, with the specific classification being: Tephroseris capitata is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, and it belongs to the genus Tephroseris. The genus Tephroseris is within the tribe Senecioneae. This classification indicates the plant's botanical relationships and its position within the plant kingdom. The classification also reflects the plant's evolutionary history and its adaptation to specific environments. As a member of the Asteraceae family, Tephroseris capitata shares characteristics with other members of this diverse and widespread plant family.
uses: Tephroseris capitata, also known as the alpine saw-wort, has been used in traditional medicine for its potential anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant's leaves and stems have been employed to treat various ailments, including wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues.
